♞Poison Pawn
Featured — World Chess Hall of Fame
Elite Mindset Training
Poison Pawn
The Quarterback Mind

Football is played on a field.
But the game is won in the mind.

Poison Pawn is a decision-making system for elite athletes. Using chess as its training ground, the method sharpens anticipation, composure, pattern recognition, and execution under pressure — the traits that separate good performers from great ones when the moment arrives.

♞  The Exhibit

Game Plan: The Strategy of Chess & Sports

World Chess Hall of Fame & Galleries · Saint Louis · On view May 7, 2026 – May 2027

Poison Pawn's performance system — including its custom-designed chess sets and training materials used with professional, Olympic, and collegiate athletes to build decision-making, focus, and composure under pressure — is featured in the exhibition, alongside artifacts connected to figures such as Garry Kasparov, Wayne Gretzky, and the St. Louis Cardinals.

In the museum's words

A performance system, recognized.

Poison Pawn uses chess to help athletes improve their decision-making under pressure. Its work spans the highest levels of sport — the NFL, NBA, MLB, and Olympic competition — as well as leaders and executives in high-performance environments. Beyond anticipation and pattern recognition, the system builds emotional resiliency: keeping one mistake from turning into more.

Its clients include UCLA Athletics, the Toronto Blue Jays, and Team USA's Artistic Swimming team — including 2024 Olympic silver medalist Anita Alvarez.

“What begins to separate the elite top performers is how they process things… how they can almost see five moves ahead.”— Game Plan exhibit, World Chess Hall of Fame
The Poison Pawn panel — Game Plan, World Chess Hall of Fame

Featured champions

The highest level of the game.

Super Bowl, World Series, and Olympic competitors who train in the Poison Pawn system.
Photograph by Bryce France
Jalen Hurts
Super Bowl Champion & MVP
Trains decision-making under pressure through the Poison Pawn system — composure, rapid adaptation, and learning through real-time mistakes, mirroring the demands faced by elite quarterbacks.
Photograph by Brandon Lawless
Mookie Betts
4× World Series Champion
Uses the Poison Pawn system to train decision-making under time pressure and uncertainty — translating the demands of the board into high-stakes moments.
Photograph courtesy of Poison Pawn
Anita Alvarez
3× Olympian · Team USA Captain
Trains with the Poison Pawn system to develop clarity, timing, and control — the attributes required to lead and perform at the highest levels of international competition.
Team USA Artistic Swimming training with the Poison Pawn system
The roster

Trained in the same system.

Authentic training on the Poison Pawn board — "Elite Mindset Training."
C. J. Stroud
Quarterback · Houston Texans
NFL Offensive Rookie of the Year
“Will you train me after this?”
Began studying strategy, processing, and decision-making under pressure — how seeing the whole board mirrors seeing the whole field.
Justin Fields
Quarterback · Kansas City Chiefs
Big Ten Offensive Player of the Year
Trained within the chess-driven performance system, focused on pattern recognition, patience, and elite decision-making.
Malik Willis
Quarterback · Miami Dolphins
Dynamic dual-threat playmaker
Used chess-based training to strengthen anticipation, discipline, and adaptability under pressure.
Dorian Thompson-Robinson
Quarterback · NFL
UCLA all-time total offense leader
“Poison Pawn Chess helped me slow the game down.”
Sharpened poise, processing speed, and leadership — translating composure from the board to the position.
Josh Dobbs
Quarterback · NFL
Aerospace engineering graduate
“At Poison Pawn, we talk about having a process.”
Used chess as a framework for slowing the game down, recognizing patterns, and making better decisions under pressure.
Sean Clifford
Quarterback · Cincinnati Bengals
Rose Bowl MVP
Trained to sharpen mental processing, patience, and decision-making as part of his leadership at the position.
Max Brosmer
Quarterback · Minnesota Vikings
Minnesota single-season completions record
Credited the training system as part of his mental growth during a record-setting season — and a chess player himself.

Quarterback · Los Angeles Chargers
Former No. 3 overall draft pick
Trained to sharpen processing and anticipation — seeing the full field before the play unfolds.
Quentin Lake
Safety · Los Angeles Rams
UCLA · defensive leader
“Poison Pawn Chess teaches you to slow down and think ahead.”
Trained as part of UCLA Football's integration of chess into athlete development.
Darnay Holmes
Defensive Back · Atlanta Falcons
UCLA team captain
“There's always an answer to the problem.”
Became deeply invested in chess and strategic thinking as part of composure and problem-solving in real time.
Jake Bobo
Wide Receiver · Seattle Seahawks
All-Pac-12 Honorable Mention
Reinforced awareness, composure, and execution under pressure through chess-based training.
Colson Yankoff

Former UCLA multi-position athlete
Explored how anticipation and preparation in chess translate to reacting in real time on the field.
Chase Griffin
Quarterback · UCLA
2× National NIL Male Athlete of the Year
“As a QB, you are in the decision-making business.”
Applied the same decision-making principles beyond football — to business, media, and leadership.
“Be a player in your own game, not a piece in someone else's.”
